 {"id":"bd-1ho.6","title":"Implement filesize formatter","description":"# Implement filesize formatter (utility)

## Objective
Provide a small utility to format byte counts in human-friendly form (binary and decimal units), useful for status lines, file pickers, and logs.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/filesize.rs`

## Scope
- Support both:
  - binary units (KiB, MiB, GiB, TiB)
  - decimal units (KB, MB, GB, TB)
- Configurable precision and short/long unit formatting
- Well-defined rounding behavior

## Tests Required
- Table-driven unit tests for edge cases:
  - 0 bytes
  - thresholds at 999/1000 and 1023/1024 boundaries
  - very large values (u64 max-ish)
  - precision/rounding correctness

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Public API exists for binary + decimal formatting and options.
- [ ] Unit tests cover boundary and rounding cases.
- [ ] Output is deterministic and documented (units + rounding rules).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:24.277137420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:00:07.442893612Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:00:07.442865199Z","close_reason":"Implemented filesize formatter + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":313,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.7","title":"Implement logging integration","description":"# Implement logging integration (rich-style formatting on top of tracing)

## Objective
Provide an optional logging layer that formats `tracing` events into styled output (Segments) in a way that is:
- readable for humans
- deterministic for tests
- compatible with ftui’s one-writer routing

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/logging.rs`

## Scope
- A formatter/subscriber (or layer) that:
  - applies per-level styling (error/warn/info/debug/trace)
  - supports timestamps (configurable)
  - optionally shows source location (file:line)
  - formats structured fields consistently

## One-Writer Rule (Critical)
- This integration must not write directly to stdout/stderr in a way that can interleave with ftui rendering.
- Output should be routed into a sink that can be:
  - captured for tests, or
  - forwarded via ftui’s `LogSink`/TerminalWriter policy.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for formatting choices (stable snapshots of formatted lines/segments).
- Concurrency test: multiple threads logging cannot interleave within a single formatted record.
- Integration test (optional) that shows logs routed into the harness log region.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A tracing integration exists (subscriber/layer) with configurable formatting.
- [ ] Output is Segment-based and can be captured deterministically in tests.
- [ ] One-writer routing is respected (no hidden direct terminal writes).
- [ ] Unit tests cover formatting and level styling.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:29.952985639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:15:48.513357374Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:15:48.513267144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":314,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.7","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.8","title":"Rich Core Infrastructure Test Suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for rich_rust core infrastructure. These foundational components support all renderables and require thorough verification.

## Test Requirements by Module

### Segment System (bd-1ho.1)
Unit tests:
- `test_segment_new_owned` - Owned string segment
- `test_segment_new_borrowed` - Borrowed string segment (Cow)
- `test_segment_control` - Control segment creation
- `test_segment_cell_length_ascii` - ASCII width = byte length
- `test_segment_cell_length_unicode` - Wide chars counted correctly
- `test_segment_cell_length_control` - Control segments = 0 width
- `test_segment_split_at_ascii` - Split ASCII segment
- `test_segment_split_at_unicode` - Split at grapheme boundary
- `test_segment_strip_styles` - Remove styles preserve text
- `test_segments_join` - Concatenate segments
- `test_segments_split_lines` - Split on newlines
- `test_segments_join_lines` - Rejoin lines

Property tests:
- `prop_split_join_inverse` - Split then join = original
- `prop_cell_length_non_negative` - Cell length >= 0
- `prop_split_preserves_total_length` - Sum of parts = whole

### Console (bd-1ho.2)
Unit tests:
- `test_console_default_width` - Width detection or fallback
- `test_console_explicit_width` - Override width
- `test_console_print_string` - Simple string output
- `test_console_print_styled` - Styled text output
- `test_console_print_renderable` - Renderable output
- `test_console_style_stack_push` - Push style
- `test_console_style_stack_pop` - Pop style
- `test_console_style_stack_nested` - Nested styles cascade
- `test_console_color_system_detect` - Auto-detect color capability
- `test_console_no_color_env` - NO_COLOR respected
- `test_console_force_color` - Force color override
- `test_console_capture_begin` - Start recording
- `test_console_capture_end` - Retrieve captured segments

Integration tests:
- `test_console_with_renderables` - Full rendering pipeline
- `test_console_line_wrapping` - Long lines wrapped

### Measurement (bd-1ho.3)
Unit tests:
- `test_measurement_new_valid` - min <= max
- `test_measurement_from_width` - min = max = width
- `test_measurement_span` - max - min
- `test_measurement_with_maximum` - Clamp maximum
- `test_measurement_with_minimum` - Clamp minimum
- `test_measure_text_single_line` - Single line width
- `test_measure_text_multiline` - Max of all lines
- `test_measure_text_with_wrap` - Account for wrap points

Property tests:
- `prop_measurement_min_le_max` - min always <= max
- `prop_measurement_clamp_valid` - Clamping preserves invariant

### Box Drawing (bd-1ho.4)
Unit tests:
- `test_box_rounded_chars` - Rounded box characters
- `test_box_square_chars` - Square box characters  
- `test_box_double_chars` - Double-line characters
- `test_box_heavy_chars` - Heavy box characters
- `test_box_ascii_chars` - ASCII fallback
- `test_box_substitute_true` - Returns ASCII variant
- `test_box_substitute_false` - Returns original
- `test_box_get_top` - Top border string
- `test_box_get_bottom` - Bottom border string
- `test_box_get_row_single` - Single cell row
- `test_box_get_row_multiple` - Multi-cell row with separators

### Live Display (bd-1ho.5)
Unit tests:
- `test_live_start` - Begin live display
- `test_live_stop` - End live display
- `test_live_update` - Replace content
- `test_live_refresh` - Redraw current content
- `test_live_vertical_overflow_ellipsis` - Truncate with ...
- `test_live_vertical_overflow_crop` - Hard crop
- `test_live_transient` - Cursor save/restore

Integration tests:
- `test_live_with_progress` - Progress bar in live context
- `test_live_nested` - Nested live displays

### Filesize Formatter (bd-1ho.6)
Unit tests:
- `test_filesize_bytes` - < 1KB shows bytes
- `test_filesize_kb_binary` - KiB formatting
- `test_filesize_mb_binary` - MiB formatting
- `test_filesize_gb_binary` - GiB formatting
- `test_filesize_tb_binary` - TiB formatting
- `test_filesize_decimal` - 1000-based units
- `test_filesize_precision` - Decimal places
- `test_filesize_short` - Short unit names
- `test_filesize_edge_zero` - Zero bytes
- `test_filesize_edge_max` - u64::MAX

Property tests:
- `prop_filesize_parses_back` - Formatted string can be parsed

### Logging Integration (bd-1ho.7)
Unit tests:
- `test_log_level_styling` - Error=red, warn=yellow, info=blue
- `test_log_timestamp_format` - Timestamp in expected format
- `test_log_source_location` - file:line shown
- `test_log_structured_fields` - Key=value pairs
- `test_log_quiet_mode` - Reduced output
- `test_log_with_live` - Logs don't corrupt live display

Integration tests:
- `test_log_tracing_subscriber` - Works with tracing

## E2E Test Scenarios

### Console Pipeline E2E
```rust
#[test]
fn e2e_console_full_render() {
    // Setup
    let mut console = Console::with_width(80);
    console.begin_capture();
    
    // Render complex content
    console.rule(Some("Header"));
    console.print(Panel::new(Text::from("Content")).with_title("Box"));
    console.print(Columns::new(vec![col1, col2]));
    console.rule(None);
    
    // Verify
    let segments = console.end_capture();
    assert!(!segments.is_empty());
    assert!(segments.iter().any(|s| s.text.contains("Header")));
    // ... more assertions
}
```

### Measurement Chain E2E
```rust
#[test]
fn e2e_measurement_chain() {
    let content = Group::new(vec![
        Box::new(Padding::new(
            Panel::new(Text::from("Inner")),
            Sides::all(2)
        )),
        Box::new(Rule::new()),
    ]);
    
    let measurement = content.measure(&console, 80);
    assert!(measurement.minimum <= measurement.maximum);
    assert!(measurement.maximum <= 80);
    
    let rendered = content.render(&console, 80);
    for line in &rendered.lines {
        let width: usize = line.iter().map(|s| s.cell_length()).sum();
        assert!(width <= 80);
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- `tracing::info!` for console output operations
- `tracing::debug!` for measurement calculations
- `tracing::trace!` for segment construction
- Live display refreshes logged at trace level

## Coverage Targets
- Line coverage: >= 90%
- Branch coverage: >= 85%

Dependencies: All Rich Core modules, Testing infrastructure (bd-10i.11)


## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Covers unit/snapshot/property tests for each rich-derived extra we keep.
- [ ] Includes at least one integration test demonstrating one-writer safe routing.
- [ ] Failure output is actionable (expected vs actual segments/lines).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:17:50.143443525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:55:47.496218399Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:55:47.496051785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":315,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening;  {"id":"bd-1i67","title":"Animation Choreographer","description":"## Overview
Provide high-level animation composition system for coordinating complex multi-element animations.

## Rationale
- Individual animations are simple but coordination is hard
- Staggered, parallel, and sequential timing needed
- Animation groups should share lifecycle
- Enables polished onboarding and transitions

## Core Components
1. **Timeline**: Sequence of animation events with timing
2. **Animation Group**: Multiple animations with shared control
3. **Stagger**: Apply delay between group members
4. **Callbacks**: Events at animation milestones
5. **Presets**: Common patterns (fade-in-group, cascade, etc.)

## Implementation Strategy
- Build on existing Animation types in ftui-widgets
- Create Choreographer in ftui-extras
- Integrate with Tick events from ftui-runtime
- Provide builder API for timeline construction

## Usage Example
Choreographer::new()
    .add(fade_in(header), Duration::ZERO)
    .add_staggered(items.map(slide_in), ms(50))
    .on_complete(|| show_content())
    .start()

## Preset Animations
- cascade_in: Staggered entrance from top
- fan_out: Elements spread from center
- typewriter: Character-by-character reveal
- pulse_sequence: Attention-grabbing highlight

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for timeline calculation
- Snapshot tests at key animation frames
- Integration tests for callback timing
- Performance tests for large animation groups

## Success Criteria
- Complex animations can be composed declaratively
- Animations stay synchronized across elements
- Preset library covers common use cases

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-1i67.1","title":"Timeline Data Structure","description":"Sequence of animation events with timing.

## Requirements
- Timeline with keyed events
- Absolute and relative timing
- Loop and repeat support
- Pause/resume/seek control

## Proposed API

```rust
pub struct Timeline<M> {
    events: Vec<TimelineEvent<M>>,
    duration: Duration,
    loop_count: LoopCount,
    state: PlaybackState,
    current_time: Duration,
}

pub struct TimelineEvent<M> {
    pub offset: Duration,       // When event starts
    pub duration: Duration,     // How long it runs
    pub animation: Box<dyn Animation<M>>,
    pub label: Option<String>,  // For seeking by name
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LoopCount {
    Once,
    Times(u32),
    Infinite,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
pub enum PlaybackState {
    Idle,
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Finished,
}

impl<M> Timeline<M> {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    /// Add event at absolute offset
    pub fn add(self, offset: Duration, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Add event relative to last event's end
    pub fn then(self, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Add event with label
    pub fn add_labeled(self, label: &str, offset: Duration, anim: impl Animation<M>) -> Self;
    
    /// Set loop behavior
    pub fn loop_count(self, count: LoopCount) -> Self;
    
    /// Playback control
    pub fn play(&mut self);
    pub fn pause(&mut self);
    pub fn resume(&mut self);
    pub fn stop(&mut self);
    pub fn seek(&mut self, time: Duration);
    pub fn seek_label(&mut self, label: &str);
    
    /// Process tick, return messages
    pub fn tick(&mut self, delta: Duration) -> Vec<M>;
    
    /// Current progress 0.0 - 1.0
    pub fn progress(&self) -> f32;
    
    /// Check if complete
    pub fn is_finished(&self) -> bool;
}
```

## Implementation Details

### Event Scheduling
Store events sorted by offset. On each tick:
1. Advance current_time by delta
2. Find events that should be active (offset <= current_time < offset + duration)
3. Call tick() on each active animation
4. Collect messages

### Looping
When current_time >= duration and loop_count allows:
- Reset current_time to 0
- Decrement loop counter (if finite)
- Reset all animations

## File: crates/ftui-extras/src/animation/timeline.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_sequential_events: A then B then C
- test_overlapping_events: Parallel animations
- test_loop_finite: Loops exact count
- test_loop_infinite: Never finishes
- test_seek_time: Jump to specific time
- test_seek_label: Jump to labeled event
- test_pause_resume: State preservation

## Snapshot Tests
- timeline_progress_0_25_50_75_100: Key frames

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Timeline Data Structure
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile; include isomorphism proof for perf changes
- Unit tests: edge cases + regression fixtures; property tests for invariants where applicable
- E2E: deterministic mode + verbose JSONL logs (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums)

 {"id":"bd-2k018.8","title":"Inline docs + usage examples for TableTheme","description":"Purpose:
- Make TableTheme self-documenting for future contributors.

Work items:
- Doc comments for TableTheme fields, presets, and effect hooks.
- Short example showing how to apply a preset + animated row highlight.
- Explicit determinism guidance: pass phase/time from caller; avoid implicit clocks.
- Explicit theme override examples (override header style, zebra, borders, selected row) with minimal imports.

Acceptance:
- A future reader can use TableTheme without referring back to planning docs.

 {"id":"bd-2k018.9","title":"Unit tests: TableTheme + effect resolver","description":"Purpose:
- Validate core TableTheme logic and effect resolver behavior independent of rendering.

Test cases:
- Base styles when no effects configured.
- Row/column/section targeting correctness.
- AllRows excludes header; AllCells includes header + body.
- Breathing/Pulse curves deterministic for fixed phase.
- Phase normalization (clamp/wrap) behaves as specified.
- Effect priority ordering + stable tie handling.
- Lerp behavior clamped to expected color bounds.
- Merge precedence: base theme vs selected/hover vs explicit Row::style vs per-cell Span style.
- Border/divider style resolution for outer vs inner borders.

Logging:
- Log phase/target/resolved colors on failure; keep tests deterministic.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover base + effect paths with deterministic expectations and actionable failure output.

 {"id":"bd-2kj","title":"Build Agent Harness reference application (ftui-harness)","description":"# Build Agent Harness Reference Application (ftui-harness)

## Background
Plan Section 12.4 and Chapter 17 define the Agent Harness as the PRIMARY design forcing function. This is not "just an example" - it is the proof that ftui works for its primary use case: Claude Code / Codex-style agent UIs.

## Purpose
The agent harness reference application demonstrates:
1. Inline mode with streaming logs and stable UI chrome
2. No flicker, no cursor corruption, reliable cleanup
3. Real-world patterns for agent harness builders

## Minimum Harness Primitives (from Plan 0.8.3)
- `write_log(text)` - scrollback-native (inline mode)
- `present_ui(frame)` - atomic and flicker-resistant
- Optional "modal alt-screen" for complex UI interactions
- Child-process capture via PTY (feature gated)

## Application Structure

```
ftui-harness/
  src/
    main.rs           # Entry point
    app.rs            # Application model
    components/
      log_viewer.rs   # Scrolling log display
      status_bar.rs   # Top status line
      tool_status.rs  # Tool execution indicator
      input_line.rs   # User input/prompt
    commands.rs       # Command handling
    pty_capture.rs    # Feature-gated PTY support
  examples/
    minimal.rs        # Bare minimum harness
    streaming.rs      # High-volume log streaming
    modal.rs          # Modal alt-screen demo
```

## UI Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Status] Model: claude-3  │  Tool: Running grep...  │  Memory: 2.1GB │ 23:45│
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                             │
│  Searching for patterns in codebase...                                     │
│  Found 42 matches in src/                                                  │
│  Analyzing results...                                                      │
│  [more log output continues...]                                            │
│                                                                             │
│                                                                             │
│                                                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ > Enter command:                                                        [⠋]│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Regions
- **Status Bar (1 line)**: Model info, tool status, memory, time
- **Log Region (variable)**: Scrolling log viewer, grows with terminal
- **Input Region (1-2 lines)**: User prompt + spinner

## Application Model

```rust
use ftui::{App, Cmd, Event, Frame, Model, ScreenMode, Result};

pub struct AgentHarness {
    // UI Components
    log_viewer: LogViewer,
    status_bar: StatusBar,
    input_line: InputLine,
    spinner: Spinner,
    
    // State
    model_name: String,
    current_tool: Option<String>,
    memory_usage: u64,
    
    // Mode
    screen_mode: ScreenMode,
    ui_height: u16,
}

impl Model for AgentHarness {
    type Message = HarnessMessage;
    
    fn init(&mut self) -> Cmd<Self::Message> {
        Cmd::batch(vec![
            Cmd::tick(Duration::from_millis(100)),  // Spinner animation
            Cmd::tick(Duration::from_secs(1)),      // Status refresh
        ])
    }
    
    fn update(&mut self, msg: HarnessMessage) -> Cmd<Self::Message> {
        match msg {
            HarnessMessage::LogLine(line) => {
                self.log_viewer.push(line);
                Cmd::none()
            }
            HarnessMessage::ToolStart(name) => {
                self.current_tool = Some(name);
                Cmd::none()
            }
            HarnessMessage::ToolEnd => {
                self.current_tool = None;
                Cmd::none()
            }
            HarnessMessage::Key(key) => {
                self.handle_key(key)
            }
            HarnessMessage::Tick => {
                self.spinner.tick();
                Cmd::none()
            }
            HarnessMessage::Quit => Cmd::quit(),
        }
    }
    
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let area = frame.buffer.area();
        
        // Status bar at top
        let status_area = Rect::new(0, 0, area.width, 1);
        self.status_bar.render(status_area, &mut frame.buffer);
        
        // Log viewer in middle
        let log_area = Rect::new(0, 1, area.width, area.height - self.ui_height);
        self.log_viewer.render(log_area, &mut frame.buffer);
        
        // Input line at bottom
        let input_area = Rect::new(0, area.height - 2, area.width, 2);
        self.input_line.render(input_area, &mut frame.buffer);
        
        // Spinner in corner
        let spinner_area = Rect::new(area.width - 2, area.height - 1, 2, 1);
        if self.current_tool.is_some() {
            self.spinner.render(spinner_area, &mut frame.buffer);
        }
    }
}
```

## Key Features to Demonstrate

### 1. Streaming Log Output
```rust
// Simulate tool output arriving in chunks
async fn stream_tool_output(tx: Sender<HarnessMessage>) {
    let output = run_tool().await;
    for line in output.lines() {
        tx.send(HarnessMessage::LogLine(line.to_string())).await?;
        // Log lines appear smoothly without flicker
    }
}
```

### 2. Inline Mode Stability
```rust
fn main() -> Result<()> {
    App::new(AgentHarness::new())
        .screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 4 })
        .run()
    // Native scrollback preserved
    // UI never corrupts scrollback
    // Cursor returns to correct position
}
```

### 3. Modal Alt-Screen (Optional)
```rust
impl AgentHarness {
    fn enter_file_picker(&mut self) -> Cmd<HarnessMessage> {
        // Temporarily switch to alt-screen for full-screen picker
        Cmd::batch(vec![
            Cmd::enter_alt_screen(),
            Cmd::msg(HarnessMessage::ShowPicker),
        ])
    }
    
    fn exit_file_picker(&mut self) -> Cmd<HarnessMessage> {
        Cmd::batch(vec![
            Cmd::exit_alt_screen(),
            Cmd::msg(HarnessMessage::PickerClosed),
        ])
    }
}
```

### 4. Panic Recovery
```rust
// Panic anywhere should restore terminal
std::panic::set_hook(Box::new(|info| {
    // Terminal cleanup happens via RAII guards
    eprintln!("Panic: {}", info);
}));
```

### 5. PTY Subprocess Capture (Feature-Gated)
```rust
#[cfg(feature = "pty")]
fn run_subprocess_with_capture() {
    let (master, slave) = openpty()?;
    let child = Command::new("cargo")
        .args(["test"])
        .stdin(slave)
        .stdout(slave)
        .stderr(slave)
        .spawn()?;
    
    // Read from master, forward to log viewer
    // All output goes through ftui (one-writer rule preserved)
}
```

## Tests Required

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Model state transitions
- [ ] Command generation
- [ ] Log viewer updates
- [ ] Status bar formatting

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Full app lifecycle (start → use → exit)
- [ ] Resize handling
- [ ] Log streaming at high volume
- [ ] Modal transitions

### PTY Tests
- [ ] Inline mode cursor contract
- [ ] Scrollback preservation
- [ ] Cleanup on exit
- [ ] Cleanup on panic
- [ ] No flicker under load

### Performance Tests
- [ ] 60 FPS with 1000 log lines
- [ ] Smooth scrolling
- [ ] Low latency input response

## Documentation
The harness should be WELL DOCUMENTED as it serves as the primary example:
- Inline comments explaining patterns
- README with usage instructions
- Architecture diagram

## Acceptance Criteria (from Plan 17.4)
- [ ] "Hello world harness" in < 200 LOC
- [ ] "Tool output streaming" is one function call
- [ ] "Pinned bottom UI" is one config option
- [ ] "Temporary full-screen modal" is supported without rewriting
- [ ] No flicker under sustained log output
- [ ] PTY tests pass (cursor, cleanup, scrollback)

## Dependencies
- Requires: bd-29v (LogViewer widget)
- Requires: bd-2dc (Status/Panel widgets)
- Requires: bd-gpe (TextInput widget)
- Requires: bd-35p (Spinner/Progress)
- Requires: bd-10i.8.2 (Runtime)
- Blocks: bd-2ky9 (E2E tests use this)

 {"id":"bd-2kn9a","title":"Diagram Type: Sequence Diagram","description":"Background
- Sequence diagrams showcase timing and interaction; great terminal demo.

Scope
- Parse sequence syntax (actors/participants, messages, notes, activations).
- Support alt/opt/loop/par/critical blocks as bordered regions.
- Lay out actors in columns with lifelines.
- Route message arrows with labels and activation bars.
- Support autonumbering and message numbering (if enabled).

Considerations
- Ensure label wrapping does not break lifelines.
- Keep spacing deterministic under size changes.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for parse + layout of representative sequences.
- Snapshot tests at multiple sizes.
- PTY E2E logs: diagram_type=sequence, size, tier, crossings, route_cost, warnings.

Success Criteria
- Clean lifelines, arrows, and notes at multiple sizes.
- Activation bars render without overlap.","notes":"Implemented SequenceMessage→IR edges/nodes; added sequence layout (actor row + message edges) and lifeline rendering + basic e2e test.

Summary of work completed:

**Parsing (mermaid.rs):**
- participant/actor declarations with aliases
- All message arrow types
- Notes (left of, right of, over)
- Activate/deactivate lifecycle
- Control blocks (loop/alt/else/opt/par/and/critical/break/rect/end)
- Autonumber

**IR Normalization:**
- IrSeqControlBlock, IrSeqNote, IrSeqActivation types
- Proper handling of unclosed blocks and activation ranges
- sequence_participants, sequence_controls, sequence_notes, sequence_activations fields

**Layout (mermaid_layout.rs):**
- Column positioning for participants
- Lifeline bounding box computation
- compute_sequence_clusters() for control block regions
- Clusters included in bounding box computation

**Rendering (mermaid_render.rs):**
- Control block frame rendering with keyword labels
- Activation bar rendering on lifelines
- Message arrows with labels

**Tests:**
- 15 parser unit tests (participant, actor, alias, notes, activations, controls, stress)
- 4 IR normalization tests (participants, notes, activations, controls)
- 2 layout tests (cluster computation, empty case)
- 6 render tests (e2e structure, basic messages, snapshots at multiple sizes)
- 10 demo snapshot tests (80x24, 120x40, 200x60 variants)

**Feature matrix** upgraded: activation bars, notes, control blocks all at Partial.

 {"id":"bd-39vx","title":"Modal/Dialog System with Proper Stacking","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI needs modals for:
- Confirmations ('Delete file?')
- Prompts ('Enter filename:')
- Complex dialogs (settings, wizards)
- Help overlay (already exists, but ad-hoc)
- Command palette (planned in bd-39y4)

Current approach: Each feature implements its own overlay logic.

## Solution: Modal Layer System

```rust
/// Modal size presets
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum ModalSize {
    Small,      // 40x10
    Medium,     // 60x20
    Large,      // 80%x80%
    FullScreen,
    Custom { width: Constraint, height: Constraint },
}

/// A modal dialog
pub struct Modal {
    pub id: ModalId,
    pub content: Box<dyn Widget>,
    pub size: ModalSize,
    pub title: Option<String>,
    pub dismissable: bool,  // Escape closes
    pub backdrop: BackdropStyle,
    pub animation: Option<ModalAnimation>,
    pub on_dismiss: Option<Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send>>,
}

/// Backdrop behind modal
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum BackdropStyle {
    None,
    Dim { opacity: f32 },  // 0.0-1.0
    Blur,  // If terminal supports
    Custom(Style),
}

/// Modal animation
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum ModalAnimation {
    FadeIn { duration: Duration },
    SlideUp { duration: Duration },
    Scale { duration: Duration },
}

/// Manages modal stack
pub struct ModalLayer {
    stack: Vec<Modal>,
    focus_manager: FocusManager,  // Uses #2
}

impl ModalLayer {
    pub fn push(&mut self, modal: Modal) -> ModalId;
    pub fn pop(&mut self) -> Option<Modal>;
    pub fn close(&mut self, id: ModalId);
    pub fn is_open(&self, id: ModalId) -> bool;
    pub fn has_modals(&self) -> bool;
    pub fn render(&self, frame: &mut Frame); // Renders all modals
}

// Convenience builders
pub fn confirm(title: &str, message: &str) -> ConfirmDialogBuilder;
pub fn prompt(title: &str, placeholder: &str) -> PromptDialogBuilder;
pub fn alert(title: &str, message: &str) -> AlertDialogBuilder;

// Usage
let result = confirm("Delete?", "This cannot be undone")
    .with_cancel("Cancel")
    .with_confirm("Delete")
    .danger()  // Red confirm button
    .show(&mut modal_layer);
```

## Visual Design

```
┌──────────── Delete File? ────────────┐
│                                       │
│  Are you sure you want to delete     │
│  'important.txt'?                    │
│                                       │
│  This action cannot be undone.       │
│                                       │
│           [Cancel]  [Delete]          │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Focus Trapping (uses #2)

- Modal pushes focus trap on open
- Tab cycles within modal
- Escape pops modal and restores focus

## Files to Create

- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/mod.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/confirm.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/prompt.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/alert.rs
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/modal_layer.rs

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Modal/Dialog System with Proper Stacking
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Formal invariants documented + enforced via property tests; any dynamic decisions have an evidence ledger and debug UI


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
- Metamorphic tests: shrink/grow, reorder non-semantic inputs, and ensure invariants still hold.

 {"id":"bd-39vx.1","title":"Modal Container Widget","description":"Core modal widget that renders content in overlay layer.

## Requirements
- Centered positioning with configurable offset
- Backdrop with configurable opacity
- Size constraints (min/max width/height)
- Close on backdrop click (optional)

## Proposed API

```rust
pub struct Modal<C: Widget> {
    content: C,
    config: ModalConfig,
    state: ModalState,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct ModalConfig {
    pub position: ModalPosition,
    pub backdrop: BackdropConfig,
    pub size: SizeConstraints,
    pub close_on_backdrop: bool,
    pub close_on_escape: bool,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub enum ModalPosition {
    Center,
    CenterOffset { x: i16, y: i16 },
    TopCenter { margin: u16 },
    Custom { x: u16, y: u16 },
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct BackdropConfig {
    pub color: Color,
    pub opacity: f32,  // 0.0 - 1.0
}

#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct SizeConstraints {
    pub min_width: Option<u16>,
    pub max_width: Option<u16>,
    pub min_height: Option<u16>,
    pub max_height: Option<u16>,
}

pub enum ModalMsg {
    Close,
    BackdropClicked,
    EscapePressed,
}
```

## Rendering Strategy
1. Render backdrop to full screen area
2. Calculate content bounds from constraints
3. Center content within available space
4. Render content on top of backdrop
5. Use overlay layer (z-index management)

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/container.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_center_calculation: Various screen sizes
- test_offset_positioning: Offset from center
- test_size_constraints: Min/max enforcement
- test_backdrop_click: Click detection on backdrop vs content

## Snapshot Tests
- modal_center_80x24, modal_offset_80x24
- modal_constrained_120x40, modal_backdrop_opacity

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Modal Container Widget
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-39vx.2","title":"Dialog Presets","description":"Common dialog patterns built on Modal.

## Requirements
- Alert dialog (message + OK button)
- Confirm dialog (message + OK/Cancel)
- Prompt dialog (message + input + OK/Cancel)
- Custom dialog builder

## Implementation
- Dialog enum with Alert, Confirm, Prompt variants
- DialogBuilder for custom dialogs
- Async result handling via Cmd

## Tests
- Each dialog type renders correctly
- Button actions work
- Input capture in prompt

## Acceptance Criteria

- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
